data.title: A trader deposited 1 million USDC into Hyperliquid to go long on Nvidia and short on Google.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, about an hour ago, a trader deposited 1 million USDC into Hyperliquid to go long on Nvidia and short on Google.

data.title: Tether's Gold Holdings Rival Central Banks, Reports Jefferies
data.description: According to Odaily, a report by Jefferies reveals that stablecoin issuer Tether currently holds 116 tons of physical gold, positioning it as one of the largest holders outside of central banks. This reserve size is comparable to the central banks of countries like South Korea, Hungary, and Greece. In the last quarter, Tether's gold purchases accounted for nearly 2% of global demand and approximately 12% of central bank purchases, potentially impacting short-term market supply and investor sentiment. Investors cited by Jefferies believe Tether plans to acquire an additional 100 tons of gold in 2025. With an anticipated profit of $15 billion this year, this goal appears attainable, according to the report. Furthermore, Tether has invested over $300 million in precious metal producers.

data.title: OwlTing Initiates $10 Million Stock Buyback Program
data.description: According to Foresight News, OwlTing, a Nasdaq-listed stablecoin infrastructure company, has announced a $10 million stock buyback program. The initiative aims to repurchase the company's Class A common stock over a nine-month period. Additionally, OwlTing revealed plans to leverage its stablecoin settlement framework to enable seamless digital currency transactions through globally recognized credit card and debit card channels.

data.title: Amber International Announces $50 Million Stock Buyback Plan
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Nasdaq-listed digital wealth management platform Amber International has announced a stock buyback plan of up to $50 million over the next 12 months. The company's board of directors approved the initiative as part of its financial strategy.

In addition, Amber International released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter of 2025. The company reported a revenue of $16.3 million, marking a significant year-over-year increase of 1,934.6%. The gross profit for the period was $11.8 million, with a gross margin of 72.3%.

The net profit from continuing operations stood at $2.15 million, while the Adjusted EBITDA was reported at $2.9 million. The platform's client assets reached $1.842 billion, reflecting a 69.8% increase compared to the previous year.

data.title: Bitwise Solana ETF withdrew 192,865 SOL from Coinbase
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Bitwise Solana ETF BSOL just withdrew 192,865 SOL from Coinbase, worth $26.39 million. It currently holds 4,317,187 SOL, worth $587 million.

data.title: Best Crypto to Buy After Bitcoin Whale Bets $2 Billion on Market Rebound
data.description:
What to Know: Bitcoin Hyper uses a modular Bitcoin L1 + SVM L2 design to deliver high-speed, low-fee smart contracts secured by Bitcoin settlement. Presale-stage projects like Bitcoin Hyper and PEPENODE offer high-beta upside if whale-led Bitcoin accumulation evolves into a full risk-on altcoin cycle. PEPENODE’s mine-to-earn concept and tiered virtual node rewards add a game layer to the memecoin thesis ahead of the next meme rotation. Shiba Inu’s Shibarium, ecosystem tokens, and ETF inclusion show how meme-origin assets can evolve into institutional-facing Web3 platforms
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/best-crypto-to-buy-after-bitcoin-whale-2b-bet/

data.title: Thailand Orders World to Delete Data Amid Privacy Concerns
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Thailand's data regulatory authority has mandated the iris-scanning project World, led by Sam Altman, to erase over 1.2 million records collected in the country. Chaichanok Chidchob, Thailand's Minister of Digital Economy and Society, stated that the Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC) also instructed the company to halt all operations in Thailand. An expert panel, tasked with investigating whether World violated Thailand's data protection laws, concluded that collecting biometric data in exchange for cryptocurrency is illegal. Consequently, World has ceased its iris-scanning activities in Thailand.

data.title: Tether holds 116 tons of gold, equivalent to the reserves of the central banks of South Korea and Hungary.
data.description: A Jefferies analysis report shows that stablecoin issuer Tether currently holds 116 tons of physical gold, making it the "largest gold holder outside of central banks," with reserves comparable to those of the central banks of countries like South Korea, Hungary, and Greece. Last quarter, Tether's gold purchases accounted for nearly 2% of global gold demand and nearly 12% of central bank purchases, and its aggressive acquisitions may have already impacted short-term market supply and investor sentiment. Jefferies cites investors who believe Tether plans to acquire another 100 tons of gold by 2025. With projected profits of $15 billion this year, this goal seems achievable. Furthermore, Tether has invested over $300 million in precious metal producers and issued the gold-backed token Tether Gold (XAUt), whose market capitalization has reached $2.1 billion, with its issuance doubling in the past six months. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Stablecoin infrastructure company OwlTing launches $10 million stock buyback program
data.description: Nasdaq-listed stablecoin infrastructure company OwlTing announced a $10 million share buyback program to repurchase its Class A common stock over a nine-month period. The company also disclosed plans to leverage its stablecoin settlement architecture to enable seamless settlement of digital currencies through globally recognized credit or debit card channels. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Bolivia Plans to Integrate Stablecoins into Financial System
data.description: According to Foresight News, Bolivia's Minister of Economy has announced plans to incorporate stablecoins into the nation's financial system. This move aims to modernize the country's financial infrastructure and enhance economic stability. The integration of stablecoins is expected to provide more secure and efficient financial transactions, aligning with global trends in digital currency adoption. Further details on the implementation timeline and specific stablecoins to be used have not been disclosed.

data.title: Thai regulators have ordered World to delete 1.2 million iris scans.
data.description: Thai regulators have ordered World to delete 1.2 million iris scans. According to Chaichanok Chidchob, Thailand's Minister of Digital Economy and Society, the company has been ordered to suspend all operations in Thailand, and an investigation has been launched into whether the company violated Thai data protection laws. (DLNews)

data.title: Digital Asset Adoption in Asia-Pacific Surpasses Global Average
data.description: According to PANews, a report by CoinDesk's Consensus titled '2025 Asia-Pacific Digital Asset Adoption Report' reveals that 24.3% of adults in the Asia-Pacific region use digital assets, significantly higher than the global average of 16.9%, totaling approximately 535 million people. The report highlights that stablecoins and cross-border remittances are the primary driving factors, with notable performance in markets such as Thailand, the UAE, and the Philippines. Among remittance users, 29% have already utilized stablecoins, with an estimated annual flow of $72 billion.

data.title: Analysis: Hedging activity surges near Bitcoin put options at $80,000
data.description: CoinDesk analyst Omkar Godbole writes that hedging activity has surged near the $80,000 put option level for Bitcoin, suggesting a potentially challenging trading environment in the short term. From a macro perspective, the strong performance of 10-year Treasury bonds, a traditional safe-haven asset, has served as a warning to other risk assets, including stocks. However, the situation could change before the end of the year, especially if the Federal Reserve cuts interest rates by 25 basis points as expected early next month, sending a clear dovish signal. (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield Declines by 5 Basis Points
data.description: According to Odaily, market reports indicate that the yield on the U.S. 10-year Treasury has decreased by 5 basis points, reaching 4.45%.

data.title: Trump Family's Crypto Project Implements Aggressive Token Buyback Strategy
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the Trump family-backed cryptocurrency initiative, World Liberty Financial, is intensifying its token buyback strategy to bolster demand for its token, which has experienced a nearly 50% decline since its inception. On Wednesday, the platform acquired $7.7 million worth of World Liberty Financial (WLFI) tokens at an average price of $0.16 per token, as reported by blockchain data platform Lookonchain. This acquisition is notably larger than the $1.06 million buyback conducted on September 27, which also involved burning $1.43 million worth of tokens.

Earlier in September, the platform approved a governance vote to allocate 100% of the project's treasury liquidity fees for token buybacks and burns, effectively removing them from circulation. Such mechanisms are designed to decrease the circulating supply of a token and enhance demand through strategic buybacks. Despite these efforts, the WLFI token has seen a 13% price increase over the past week but remains down by over 48% since its launch in January, according to CoinMarketCap data.

U.S. President Donald Trump's family reportedly saw their stake soar to approximately $5 billion following the scheduled unlock of 24.6 billion tokens at the beginning of September. However, the Trump family's cryptocurrency ventures faced a substantial setback after the record $19 billion crypto market crash on October 10. Their fortune has diminished by over a billion dollars since the crash, dropping from around $7.7 billion in early September to $6.7 billion by the end of November, as reported by Bloomberg.

At present, the WLFI-tagged wallet address continues to hold $7.5 billion worth of WLFI tokens as its largest asset, according to blockchain data platform Arkham. Cryptocurrency endeavors have proven profitable for the Trump family, generating approximately $802 million in cryptocurrency income during the first half of 2025, according to estimates from Reuters. The investigation revealed that the majority of this income originated from WLFI tokens, the Official Trump (TRUMP) memecoin, and passive yield from the World Liberty Financial USD (USD1) stablecoin.

data.title: Amber International launched a $50 million share buyback program and disclosed Q3 revenue of $16.3 million.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed digital wealth management platform Amber International's board of directors approved a 12-month share repurchase program of up to $50 million and announced unaudited results for the third quarter of 2025: revenue of $16.3 million, up 1,934.6% year-over-year; gross profit of $11.8 million, with a gross margin of 72.3%; net income from continuing operations of $2.15 million; adjusted EBITDA of $2.9 million; and client assets on the platform of $1.842 billion, up 69.8% year-over-year.

data.title: Binance Contributes to Cyber Patrol – Global Operation Targeting Financial Lifelines of Digital Piracy
data.description:
Main TakeawaysBinance supported Europol, EUIPO, and leading audiovisual and crypto sector partners in Cyber Patrol, a coordinated week-long global operation to dismantle digital piracy networks. Instead of chasing individual pirate sites, the mission targeted the financial infrastructure powering illegal IPTV and streaming ecosystems. Cyber Patrol demonstrates that data-driven, cross-border collaboration is the most effective method for disrupting organized digital piracy. Digital piracy has evolved into a resilient, borderless ecosystem — a constantly morphing network of illegal IPTV platforms, covert streaming services, and subscription-reselling operations
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/32904844844657?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS
